title: Charred Spanish Onion Tortilla Espanola
description: A rich and savory Spanish dish featuring charred spring onions and a crispy fried exterior, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner. Tortilla espanola, a Spanish omelette, is a staple of Valencian cuisine.
instructions: Heat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Char the onions until they're well-browned and slightly blackened, about 10 minutes per side. Stir occasionally to prevent burning.
Let the onions cool, then finely chop them and set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, smoked paprika, black pepper, and salt.
Dust a plate with flour and set aside.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the mixture to the skillet and cook until the edges start to set, about 2-3 minutes.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the tortilla towards the center, allowing the uncooked egg to flow to the edges.
Continue cooking and folding the tortilla for another 2-3 minutes, until almost set.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the tortilla is golden brown and cooked through.
Slide the tortilla onto the prepared plate and let it cool for a minute or two before slicing and serving.
